title: Upcoming Holiday Hours
content_md:
# Upcoming Holiday Hours

- Code: 6acce0ae
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=6acce0ae05194adb9ff94c2e3aa46cf0
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/4dda82f0b4384a43a9186d884bfffb12?sid=a9461371-e5b4-469d-b060-3b764f807068?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 1m 6s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So let's talk about holiday hours.
If you come into your Google Business Profile,
hit Edit Profile, come down to Hours,
you can see it under Special Hours.
We need to keep this updated.
This is one of the factors that Google uses to see
if you care about your GVP is whether you have
Special Hours, Holiday Hours entered.
So we'll hit this pencil and then we can edit them.
And you can see right now, the farthest one we can edit
is the Fourth of July.
So we'll definitely want to put in Fourth of July Hours,
but we can't put in Thanksgiving or Christmas
or New Year's Hours yet.
It only goes a couple of months out.
So the way we manage this is my team member
who keeps all of our client GVPs updated.
She has a calendar entry in her calendar
that goes off every other month,
on the first of the month, and it just says,
hey, go in and update Holiday Hours for all of our clients.
It's kind of annoying that we have to do it that way,
but it's important to do it,
important to keep these hours up to date,
shows Google that your GVP is up to date
and active.
Also, if you have a Bing Places listing,
do the same thing for Bing as for Google.

transcript_text: So let's talk about holiday hours. If you come into your Google Business Profile, hit Edit Profile, come down to Hours, you can see it under Special Hours. We need to keep this updated. This is one of the factors that Google uses to see if you care about your GVP is whether you have Special Hours, Holiday Hours entered. So we'll hit this pencil and then we can edit them. And you can see right now, the farthest one we can edit is the Fourth of July. So we'll definitely want to put in Fourth of July Hours, but we can't put in Thanksgiving or Christmas or New Year's Hours yet. It only goes a couple of months out. So the way we manage this is my team member who keeps all of our client GVPs updated. She has a calendar entry in her calendar that goes off every other month, on the first of the month, and it just says, hey, go in and update Holiday Hours for all of our clients. It's kind of annoying that we have to do it that way, but it's important to do it, important to keep these hours up to date, shows Google that your GVP is up to date and active. Also, if you have a Bing Places listing, do the same thing for Bing as for Google.
